documentation.value: Prints a message to the console.
The primary message or value to log
Additional values to log, formatted using substitution strings if applicable

signatures.label: setTimeout(callback: (...args: any[]) => void, ms?: number, ...args: any[]): NodeJS.Timeout

documentation.value: Schedules a function to be called after a specified delay in milliseconds.
The function to call after the timer elapses
The delay in milliseconds (default: 0)
Additional arguments to pass to the callback

signatures.label: setInterval(callback: (...args: any[]) => void, ms?: number, ...args: any[]): NodeJS.Timeout

documentation.value: Repeatedly calls a function with a fixed time delay between each call.
The function to call repeatedly
The interval in milliseconds between calls
Additional arguments to pass to the callback

signatures.label: fetch(input: RequestInfo | URL, init?: RequestInit): Promise<Response>

documentation.value: Starts the process of fetching a resource from the network, returning a promise that resolves to the Response.
The resource URL or a `Request` object
Optional request configuration: `method`, `headers`, `body`, `mode`, `credentials`, etc.

signatures.label: JSON.stringify(value: any, replacer?: (key: string, value: any) => any | (string | number)[] | null, space?: string | number): string

documentation.value: Converts a JavaScript value to a JSON string.
The value to convert to a JSON string
A function or array that transforms the results, or null
Indentation: a number of spaces or a string (up to 10 characters)

signatures.label: JSON.parse(text: string, reviver?: (key: string, value: any) => any): any

documentation.value: Parses a JSON string and returns the resulting JavaScript value.
The JSON string to parse
Optional function to transform each parsed key-value pair

signatures.label: Array<T>.map<U>(callbackfn: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => U, thisArg?: any): U[]

documentation.value: Creates a new array populated with the results of calling a provided function on every element.
A function called for every element: `(value, index, array) => newValue`
Optional value to use as `this` when executing the callback

signatures.label: Array<T>.filter(predicate: (value: T, index: number, array: T[]) => boolean, thisArg?: any): T[]

documentation.value: Creates a new array with all elements that pass the test implemented by the provided function.
A function to test each element: `(value, index, array) => boolean`
Optional value to use as `this` when executing the predicate

signatures.label: Array<T>.reduce<U>(callbackfn: (accumulator: U, currentValue: T, currentIndex: number, array: T[]) => U, initialValue: U): U

documentation.value: Executes a reducer function on each element of the array, resulting in a single output value.
A reducer function: `(accumulator, currentValue, currentIndex, array) => newAccumulator`
The initial value for the accumulator
Takes an iterable of promises and returns a single promise that resolves when all input promises resolve.
An iterable of promises or values
Returns an array of the given object's own enumerable string-keyed property names.
The object whose property names to retrieve

signatures.label: Object.assign<T, U>(target: T, source: U): T & U

documentation.value: Copies all enumerable own properties from one or more source objects to a target object and returns the target.
The target object to copy properties to
One or more source objects to copy properties from
Constructs an object type whose property keys are `Keys` and whose property values are `Type`.
The union of string literal types or `string` for the property keys
The type of the property values
Constructs a type with all properties of `Type` set to optional.
The type to make all properties optional
Constructs a type by picking the specified set of properties from `Type`.
The type to pick properties from
The union of string literal types naming the properties to pick
Constructs a type by picking all properties from `Type` and then removing `Keys`.
The source type
The union of string literal types naming the properties to omit
